reasoning: With ~10 months until the resolution date (Dec 31, 2025), predicting the exact #1 model on a specific day relies heavily on the unpredictable release cadence of the major AI labs. Currently, the landscape contains 4-5 top contenders with the capability to hit #1: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, DeepSeek, and xAI. Anthropic has a strong track record of periodically claiming the #1 spot (e.g., with Claude 3 Opus and Claude 3.5 Sonnet). However, with OpenAI's massive resources, Google's rapid iteration on Gemini, DeepSeek's recent breakthrough with highly performant models, and xAI's massive compute clusters, the competition is increasingly fierce. Assigning a fair-share base rate among these 5 players yields ~20%. I adjust slightly upwards to 23% because Anthropic has a structural advantage in alphabetical tie-breakers against almost all competitors (DeepSeek, Google, OpenAI, xAI), and they are highly competent at producing top-ranking models.
